notes: Leaves data sources in place; real-time ETL; cross-data-source querying without copying; creates global metadata repository; distinct from EAI and ETL; market <$200M in 2003
Mature predecessor to EII; combine data migration and replication; target data warehouse market then EAI; suppliers: Compuware, Data Junction, ETI, Sybase, TIBCO, webMethods
EAI market $2.2B (2002) growing to $3.0B (2004); many ETL tools packaged as part of EAI; Aberdeen segments EAI as complementary to EII

Descendant of SGML; sibling of HTML; flexible data portability; XML databases as persistent cache; SonicMQ, Software AG among providers; Aberdeen expects all EII suppliers to wrap transactions in XML within 2 years
Largest aggregation of data and metadata in enterprise; limitations: performance at large size; query-only; data fiefdom resistance; daily refresh constraint; all major DB suppliers support
Combines key data from multiple sources; allows updating unlike data warehouse; near real-time freshness; pioneered by Unisys; now generally accepted
Recent idea; high-performance database as persistent cache for multiple data sources; examples: Raining Data TigerLogic XDMS, Ipedo Information Hub; complements EII
Idea whose time has passed per Aberdeen; provides same DB software to different data sources; failed due to difficulty making one operation fit all data sources; Informix federated approach noted
Portal market $1.5B (2002) to $2.8B (2004); EII required for portals to show more than data side-by-side; DataChannel, Sequoia Software, iPlanet among portal providers

EII-type functionality cornerstone of web services; Web-servicized EII provides cross-company data access; Aberdeen expects XML wrap for all EII suppliers within 2 years; HP, IBM, Sun, Microsoft as web services providers
Product from IBM Xperanto EII project; IBM $60M EII revenue estimate; dramatic visibility growth due to IBM announcement
Full-featured EII solution; MetaBase modeling tool (MOF-based); virtual databases; XA-compliant transactions; Java-built for scalability; SOAP/XML/ODBC/JDBC; OEM agreement with SAP NetWeaver
BEA contracting with Enosys to provide EII; EII incorporated in application server; BEA $25M EII revenue estimate
Content management market $1.2B (2002) to $2.0B (2004) per Aberdeen; EII increasingly incorporated in content management solutions
EJB 2.0 components; simplifies data access; EII a complement; EII solution providers (incl. MetaMatrix) built entirely in Java for scalability; J2EE application servers
